subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused, and consistently guides the investigation
Topic is communicated accurately with clear biological and economic context
Appropriate range of secondary sources and graphical data support the exploration
weaknesses: Minor drift into broader theoretical background not strictly needed for the RQ
Lacks an explicit methodological rationale explaining source selection
No primary data collection or triangulation to deepen methodological completeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student presents a clearly defined, narrow research question that frames the entire essay. Explanations of ecological and economic background are accurate and effective. A variety of relevant secondary sources and data visualizations demonstrate purposeful selection. However, the methodology section is under-developed: there is no explicit rationale for choosing sources, no primary research, and occasional off-topic theory sections. These minor gaps justify a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Graphs and tables are integrated with relevant commentary
Key biological and economic concepts are defined and applied correctly
Interdisciplinary framing shows coherent understanding of both environmental and market dimensions
weaknesses: Some figures are inserted without in-depth analysis or complete citations
Reliance on secondary summaries limits depth of original insight
Occasional terminology inconsistencies (mixing abbreviations and minor language slips)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates clear, coherent application of source material and mostly accurate use of terminology. Charts and economic tables are interpreted to support arguments, and interdisciplinary concepts are woven throughout. Yet the depth of analysis varies, citations are sometimes incomplete, and a few language inconsistencies persist. These qualities align with the upper middle band, meriting 4 out of 6.

strengths: Logical, coherent argument structure linking evidence to claims
Good relevance of analysis to the research question, with effective use of quantitative data
Balanced discussion weighing environmental and economic trade-offs
weaknesses: Analysis can be descriptive rather than deeply causal in places
Evaluation is partially supported, with some assertions lacking critical testing
Minor logical leaps and redundancy reduce overall sharpness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay offers a generally clear and coherent argument, with quantitative evidence (catch trends, quotas) tied to economic theory and sustainability concepts. Discussion of trade-offs shows thoughtful evaluation. However, analysis sometimes remains at a descriptive level, and counter-arguments receive only brief treatment. This performance falls into the lower half of the 7â9 band, so a score of 7 out of 12 is appropriate.

strengths: Clear overall structure with comprehensive table of contents and numbered sections
Effective use of maps, figures, and captions to support spatial and quantitative understanding
Consistent section headings aid reader navigation
weaknesses: Inconsistent indentation and numbering in the table of contents
Several figures lack full captions or source details
Minor formatting errors (truncated URLs, stray text) detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally effective: the report is well-structured, includes necessary front matter, and employs visuals to good effect. Nevertheless, inconsistencies in layout, figure captions, and citation formatting prevent a top score. The work aligns with a solid middle performance in presentation, warranting 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: A full table of contents, numbered sections, introduction, body and conclusion are present and logically ordered.  Headings, sub-headings and page numbers aid navigation, meeting the expectations for an EE.  Minor issues such as misplaced subsections and inconsistently labelled figures do not seriously impede clarity, so the higher band is appropriate.

The student includes word count, title, page numbers and uses tables, images and captions.  Citation style is broadly consistent, but several URLs are truncated, superscript numbering is irregular, and some figures lack full source details.  Overall layout is functional and mostly correct, yet the noted inconsistencies prevent the maximum score.

The essay follows a logical structureâbackground, environmental analysis, economic analysis, improvementsâand the discussion usually flows from evidence to claim, indicating a coherent argument.  Some redundancy (repeated quota narratives) and minor logical leaps reduce overall sharpness, so while the argument is clear and mostly consistent, it is not polished enough for 7â9 band.

The student introduces the sustainability of Mediterranean bluefin tuna clearly, gives biological and economic context, and explains why it is significant locally and internationally. The explanation is generally accurate and covers historical background, ecological role and economic value. However, depth is uneven (e.g., social pillar only briefly noted) and some passages become narrative rather than explanatory, so the strand does not fully reach the comprehensive standard of the top band.

Graphs of catches, TAC and economic tables are integrated and usually interpreted, indicating relevant application.  Yet some figures are merely inserted with brief commentary; citations are sometimes incomplete; and the student relies heavily on secondary summaries rather than analysing original datasets.  The knowledge displayed is coherent but not consistently deep, positioning the work in the middle band.

The student analyses catch trends against quotas, links economic theory diagrams to real data, and compares historical and current stock status, showing clear relevance to the RQ.  Analysis is more descriptive in places (e.g., repeating quota changes without deeper causal exploration) and occasionally conflates Mediterranean and global contexts, so quality is good but not sustained enough for the top two bands.

A range of secondary sources (FAO, ICCAT, Ifremer, National Geographic, academic concepts such as the tragedy of the commons) is consulted and graphical data are presented, showing some purposeful selection.  Nevertheless the method is largely desk-based web research with no primary data collection, little explanation of selection criteria, and minimal triangulation.  The bibliography shows repetition and several popular-media sites, indicating only partial completeness.

There is a reasonable attempt to weigh the effectiveness of ICCAT measures and to discuss economic-environment trade-offs, culminating in a balanced conclusion.  Supporting evidence is provided but sometimes asserted rather than critically tested, and counter-arguments (e.g., social impacts, illegal fishing bias in data) are only briefly noted.  Hence evaluation is adequate and partially supported, aligning with the mid-band score of 4.

The research questionââDans quelle mesure la pÃªche du thon rougeâ¦ est-elle Ã©conomiquement et environnementalement durable?ââis explicitly stated on the first page, narrowly restricted to one species and one geographical region, and links two clear dimensions (economics and biology).  Throughout the essay the student keeps returning to quotas, stock trends and market forces, showing that the question guides the work.  Minor drift occurs when long descriptive sections on development-sustainability theory add material not strictly needed for the RQ, so a top-band 6 is not justified.

Key biological terms (stock, quota, TAC) and economic concepts (externalities, supplyâdemand shifts, tragedy of the commons) are defined and applied largely correctly.  Occasional language slips (âchaireâ for âchairâ, mixing English and French abbreviations) occur, but overall terminology is used mostly accurately and consistently enough for the upper end of the middle band.

annotations.comment: Analysis of catch trends against quotas is descriptive; incorporate percentage overage calculations to deepen the analysis.
Footnotes are fragmented across lines and lack consistent numbering; consolidate into a uniform citation style.
Indentation and formatting of sub-sections in the TOC are inconsistent; standardize numbering and indentation to improve clarity.
The title is clearly stated, meeting the requirement and providing a focused investigation topic.
The word count is provided and remains under the 4 000-word limit, demonstrating compliance with formatting guidelines.
The table of contents is comprehensive and shows a logical structure, aiding navigation through the investigation.
The research question is prominently stated at the start of the introduction, effectively framing the scope of the essay.
The raw URL in the footnote could be replaced by a formatted citation (author, date, title) to enhance professionalism.
The introduction contextualizes the topic but lacks an explicit rationale for source selection; include a brief methodology statement to clarify approach.
The interdisciplinary framing linking biological and economic aspects demonstrates clarity in communicating the topicâs relevance.
The claim that the species does not meet sustainability criteria is unsupported; cite specific benchmarks or frameworks (e.g., Brundtland).
The sustainable development section clearly explains the three pillars, demonstrating solid conceptual knowledge.
Footnote numbering and formatting for sustainability references are inconsistent; revise to a uniform citation scheme.
When discussing changes in quotas, evaluate the impact of policy decisions versus enforcement effectiveness for critical depth.
The geographical distribution map effectively visualizes the tunaâs range, supporting spatial understanding.
The caption for global usage is incomplete; expand it to describe the data shown and cite the source explicitly.
The definition of a fish stock is accurately presented with appropriate terminology and citation.
Clarify terminology when distinguishing Mediterranean and Pacific stocks by defining âsub-stockâ versus âspeciesâ consistently.
The figure illustrating stock diagnostics lacks a caption and source detail; include these for academic completeness.
Include a brief methodological note on how data sources were selected and retrieved to enhance transparency and replication.
The generic IUCN categories table is extensive but not directly analyzed; focus on the specific âEndangeredâ designation and its implications.
The interpretation of the IUCN âEndangeredâ status is accurate and linked to management responses.
The stray text âcheâ appears inadvertently; remove or correct this to maintain professional presentation.
The landing data figure group effectively demonstrates stock recovery and integrates quantitative evidence.
The description of sustainable fishing principles is thorough but not tied to a specific case study; integrate examples of these principles in action.
Figures are included without descriptive captions; add legend descriptions explaining axes, units, and highlighted trends.
The historical âbroumÃ©â example enriches the narrative by illustrating traditional methods and their ecological impact.
Format the MSC source as a proper citation with author and date rather than a bare URL to enhance scholarly rigor.
